body_txt: A person sees or hears something they find so infuriating, shocking, saddening, exciting, or otherwise discombobulating that they suddenly fall physically ill, sometimes collapse or fall unconscious, and sometimes die. The illness is most often said to be or depicted as a Hollywood Heart Attack , a stroke, or an aneurysm, but others are possible, such as a Convulsive Seizure , asthma attack , vomiting , fainting , or miscarriage . Family and friends of the possible victim can also warn them against this possibility to try to keep them from getting worked up (“Careful, dear, your blood pressure!”) Sometimes happens in the middle of delivering an angry rant . Sometimes heralded by a Dramatic Drop : the dish drops, then the person does. This may serve as the culmination of a Villainous Breakdown . Indeed, this can be especially convenient for authors who need to remove a villainous character but don’t want the good characters to be morally compromised by killing them : the hero simply triumphs and/or gives a sufficiently eloquent speech , and the villain’s resulting rage causes them to die all on their own. In fantastic contexts, characters can suffer a kind of magical equivalent of a heart attack: they get so mad that they suffer some sort of involuntary, emotionally driven Power Incontinence that makes them explode , vanish, or otherwise magically die . This trope can also be used on offense, as a lethally exaggerated version of the Emotion Bomb . Truth in Television : sudden intense emotion affects bodily systems such as blood pressure and can trigger conditions such as epileptic seizures and heart attacks, usually in people with an underlying vulnerability ("diathesis") or pre-existing condition. In times past, an “apoplexy” was any sudden illness and death that started with an abrupt loss of consciousness, often strokes but also aneurysms or even heart attacks. This is where we get the adjective “apoplectic” meaning “extremely angry”: literally, so mad you collapse and die (similar to other hyperboles for shock and anger, like "Mom will have an aneurysm when she sees this!" or "Are you trying to give me a heart attack?!"). There's also takotsubo cardiomyopathy , a disorder in which emotional trauma weakens the heart muscle. Not quite a subtrope, but close, of Your Mind Makes It Real and Brown Note . Subtropes include Stress Vomit , Faint in Shock , Laugh Themselves Sick , Brain Fever , and (in supernatural settings) Angst Nuke ; Angst Coma , the arousal-induced Nosebleed , and Bring My Brown Pants (fear-induced incontinence) are cousins. This trope overlaps with Fright Deathtrap (in cases where the victim is simply frightened to death, rather than frightened into doing something that kills them), Die Laughing (in cases where the person dies from laughing, not just while laughing), and Dumb Struck (where the inability to speak is persistent). Death by Despair is closely related but not necessarily sudden. When the emotion leads to sudden mental illness, that's Go Mad from the Revelation . Milder relatives (emotions affecting physiology in a non-pathological way) are Cross-Popping Veins (suggesting high blood pressure in response to anger or frustration but not usually accompanied by illness), Dramatic Sit-Down , Lost My Appetite , Too Unhappy to Be Hungry , and Prematurely Grey-Haired . Since this is often a Death Trope , spoilers are unmarked. Beware!
